title: Semantic Structure — HTML5 Sections
description: Replace generic div containers with semantic HTML5 elements. Use <header> for the top area with your name and navigation, <nav> for the navigation links, <main> to wrap the primary content, <section> for each content area, and <footer> at the bottom with copyright info.
starter_code:
# Portfolio Website — Step 8: Semantic HTML5 Elements # Replace divs with header, nav, main, section, and footer. html = """ My Portfolio

Your Name

About Me | Projects | Contact

About Me

I am a developer who is passionate about building things. I am constantly learning and love problem-solving.

SkillLevelYears
HTMLBeginner1
CSSBeginner1
PythonIntermediate2

My Projects

Contact

""" print(html)
test_code:
assert '<header>' in html.lower() and '</header>' in html.lower(), 'Must include a <header> element'
assert '<nav>' in html.lower() and '</nav>' in html.lower(), 'Must include a <nav> element for navigation'
assert '<main>' in html.lower() and '</main>' in html.lower(), 'Must include a <main> element for primary content'
assert '<section' in html.lower(), 'Must use <section> elements for content areas'
assert html.lower().count('<section') >= 2, f'Need at least 2 <section> elements, found {html.lower().count("<section")}'
assert '<footer>' in html.lower() and '</footer>' in html.lower(), 'Must include a <footer> element'
acceptance_criteria: A header element wraps the site title and navigation
A nav element contains the navigation links
A main element wraps the primary page content
A footer element is at the bottom of the page
